raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a XXXX TV from Best Buy for {$1600.00} using my Best Buy credit card. I returned this TV on XX/XX/XXXX and exchanged it for a different model, as documented in my XX/XX/XXXX receipt. 

Despite the return, my account was billed for both televisions. The original {$1600.00} charge was never properly reversed, leaving me responsible for two TVs when I only kept one.

In my prior CFPB complaint, Citi responded by claiming I received an {$840.00} refund applied toward a new purchase of {$2500.00}, leaving a balance of {$1700.00}. This explanation is incorrect and misleading : My account still shows the {$1600.00} charge, unreversed.

The documentation clearly shows a return and exchange.

I am not disputing the {$1700.00} balance for the TV I actually kept only the duplicate {$1600.00} charge.

This is a clear billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ). Citi failed to investigate and resolve the duplicate charge properly.

Requested Resolution : Remove the {$1600.00} duplicate charge from my account.

Correct my billing statements to show only the TV I retained.

Provide written confirmation that my account balance and credit report are corrected.

Bottom line : I returned one TV and should not have to pay for two. The {$1600.00} charge must be removed.
